Skorupi

#0450 Hippowdon #0452 Drapion
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Skorupi is a Poison/Bug type Pokémon introduced in Generation 4.

Skorupi

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0451
Type Poison Bug
Species Scorpion Pokémon
Height 0.8 m (2′07″)
Weight 12.0 kg (26.5 lbs)
Abilities 1. Battle Armor
2. Sniper
Keen Eye (hidden ability)
Local № 0127 (Diamond/Pearl)
0127 (Platinum)
0201 (Black 2/White 2)
0015 (X/Y — Mountain Kalos)
0285 (Sword/Shield)
0127 (Brilliant Diamond/Shining Pearl)
0148 (Legends: Arceus)
0050 (The Isle of Armor)

Training

EV yield 1 Defense
Catch rate 120 (15.7% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 50 (normal)
Base Exp. 66
Growth Rate Slow

Breeding

Egg Groups Bug, Water 3
Gender 50% male, 50% female
Egg cycles 20 (4,884–5,140 steps)

Base stats

HP 40
190 284
Attack 50
94 218
Defense 90
166 306
Sp. Atk 30
58 174
Sp. Def 55
103 229
Speed 65
121 251
Total 330 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

Evolution chart

#0451
Skorupi
Poison · Bug
(Level 40)
#0452
Drapion
Poison · Dark

Skorupi changes

  • In Generation 4, Skorupi has a base experience yield of 114.
  • In Generations 4-7, Skorupi has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Diamond It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.
Pearl It lives in arid lands. It buries itself in sand and lies in wait for unsuspecting prey.
Platinum As soon as the tail claws close, its needle tips secrete poison. It can survive a year without food.
HeartGold
SoulSilver		 It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.
Black
White		 As soon as the tail claws close, its needle tips secrete poison. It can survive a year without food.
Black 2
White 2		 It lives in arid regions and can go without food for a year while waiting for prey.
X
Omega Ruby		 It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.
Y
Alpha Sapphire		 It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.
Sword After burrowing into the sand, it waits patiently for prey to come near. This Pokémon and Sizzlipede share common descent.
Shield It attacks using the claws on its tail. Once locked in its grip, its prey is unable to move as this Pokémon’s poison seeps in.
Brilliant Diamond It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.
Shining Pearl It lives in arid lands. It buries itself in sand and lies in wait for unsuspecting prey.
Legends: Arceus Its claws are not only razor-sharp but poisonous, making Skorupi a highly dangerous Pokémon. It seems to be weakened by cold temperatures, however.

Legends: Z-A Legends: Arceus

Moves learnt by level up

Skorupi does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Skorupi cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by level up

Skorupi learns the following moves in Pokémon Legends: Arceus at the levels specified.

Move
Type
Cat.
Power
Acc.

Platinum		 Great Marsh

White		 Trade/migrate from another game

White 2		 Reversal Mountain

Y		 Route 14, 15, 16

Alpha Sapphire		 Route 114

Moon		 Trade/migrate from another game

Shield		 Route 6, Giant's Mirror, Motostoke Riverbank, Soothing Wetlands

Other languages

English Skorupi
Japanese スコルピ (Scorupi)
German Pionskora
French Rapion
Italian Skorupi
Spanish Skorupi
Korean 스콜피 (seukolpi)
Chinese (Simplified) 钳尾蝎
Chinese (Traditional) 鉗尾蠍

 

English Scorpion Pokémon
Japanese さそりポケモン
German Skorpion-Pokémon
French Pokémon Scorpion
Italian Pokémon Scorpione
Spanish Pokémon Escorpión
Korean 전갈포켓몬
Chinese (Simplified) 蝎子宝可梦
Chinese (Traditional) 蠍子寶可夢

Name origin

scorpion
creature with pincers and a stinger in its tail
#0450 Hippowdon #0452 Drapion
